untranslatable_reason: This signal is a CONTRACT ROLL rule, not a directional trading signal. It specifies when to roll futures contracts (fixed calendar date or volume/open interest switch), which is a position management/structural operation, not a price-based entry/exit condition. The allowed indicators and operators are designed for price/technical analysis, not contract metadata (delivery month, contract expiration, open interest switches). Implementation requires: (1) calendar logic to identify last day of month before delivery month, (2) contract-level open interest/volume data comparison across contract months, and (3) roll execution logic—none of which fit the entry/exit condition schema.
translation_notes: Signal KAUFMAN_DRY_21 describes a roll methodology with two options: (a) fixed date based on delivery month calendar, (b) dynamic trigger based on open interest/volume migration. Both require access to futures contract specifications and inter-contract volume/OI data. This is a structural/operational rule best implemented as a separate roll module in the backtest engine, not as price-based entry/exit conditions. Recommend handling as a dedicated contract_roll_rule object outside the standard condition-based signal framework.
